sessions.intent: A gentle, restorative 30-minute rebuild for postpartum women aged 30-40. No Sweat means the heart rate barely lifts: held shapes, slow tempo, generous rest, and breath leading every move. The spine is the entry point, the core is reconnected through breath rather than crunching (diastasis-recti-safe: no crunches, sit-ups, full planks, or jumping), and the session closes with deep down-regulation. This is built to be a safe FIRST option in the return to movement — the goal is showing up and feeling steadier in your own body, never intensity, never appearance.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome. You are exactly where you need to be. Come to a comfortable seat, let your hands rest wherever feels easy, and let your eyes soften or close. There is nothing to chase here. We are going to begin with the breath, settle into the body you have today, and move slowly together. If anything ever feels like too much, you have full permission to pause or to skip it. Let us begin.
sessions.veraClose: You came, you breathed, you moved with kindness — and that is the whole thing. Notice how your body feels right now, a little more settled, a little more yours. Nothing about today was about fixing you, because you were never broken. Rest here a moment longer. The garden grows because you showed up, and you did. Beautifully done.

items.dose: 8 slow breath cycles, ~4s inhale / ~6s exhale, seated and supported, ~60s
items.tierNote: No Sweat arrival. pelvic-breath is the core-connection anchor for this tier and a vetted seated breath; opening with it satisfies the mandatory seated-breath arrival and primes the deep core before any load. Restorative, not exertional.

items.dose: 40s, slow small half-circles, ~6-8s per pass, no holds
items.tierNote: No Sweat dose: smaller range, lazy pace. Releases the neck tension carried from feeding and carrying before any upper-body work.

items.dose: 40s, small lazy circles, ~5s each
items.tierNote: No Sweat dose: small lazy circles, arms hanging heavy. Warms the shoulder girdle gently; no temperature-raising brisk pace at this tier.

items.dose: 50s, one full wave per breath, ~5-6s each, slow and exploratory
items.tierNote: Used here as a warm-up (blocks: warmup/main). No Sweat: slow and exploratory, reconnecting belly and breath — the kindest way to wake the spine postpartum.

items.dose: 50s, small slow standing circles, ~6s each, switching direction at halfway
items.tierNote: No Sweat dose: small circles, gentle dynamic lower-body warm-up. No brisk/large circles, which belong to higher tiers.

items.dose: 50s, 3-4 unhurried full roll-down / roll-up waves, ~10-12s per wave
items.tierNote: No Sweat held tempo, breath-paced. Bridges the warm-up into the main block by mobilising the whole spine slowly; not eligible above slightly_sweaty, perfect for restorative pacing.

items.dose: 8 cycles, inhale-soften ~4s / exhale-lift-and-hold 2-3s / full release, no rest, ~60s
items.tierNote: Connection primer opening the main block (blocks: main/warmup/close). Establishes the breath-and-pelvic-floor coordination that makes the subsequent gentle loading safe. Restorative, not a sweat driver. [evidence: pelvic health physiotherapy consensus supports breath-coordinated pelvic-floor connection early postpartum].

items.dose: No Sweat: 8 reps, 2-3s up / 2s hold / 3s down, rest 45s
items.tierNote: Diastasis-recti-safe posterior-chain strength and a core-safe alternative to floor crunch work. No Sweat dose with the longest (45s) rest of the tier. [evidence: ACOG postpartum exercise guidance supports gradual resumption of strengthening; bridges are a standard DR-safe choice].

items.dose: No Sweat: 60s settled per side, back knee padded, breath-led
items.tierNote: Soft/mobility counterpart alternated after strength. No Sweat: fully settled, longer hold, back knee padded for comfort. Sided — both sides cued.

items.dose: No Sweat: 5 reps per side, reach 3s / hold 2s / return 3s, rest 30s
items.tierNote: Core stability without spinal flexion — the flagship DR-safe core move (replaces planks/crunches). No Sweat: lowest reps, slow tempo, 30s rest. Sided.

items.dose: No Sweat: 50s per side, gentle and supported, twist a little further on each exhale
items.tierNote: Soft/mobility counterpart. No Sweat: 50s, gentle and supported, breath-led. Sided. Not eligible above slightly_sweaty — ideal restorative pacing.

items.dose: No Sweat: 10 reps per side, open 2s / close 2s, rest 30s
items.tierNote: Gentle glute-medius / lateral-hip strength, low-load and pelvis-friendly. No Sweat dose, 30s rest. Sided.

items.dose: No Sweat: 60s per side, fully supported on the floor
items.tierNote: Soft/stretch counterpart and a hip release after lateral hip work. No Sweat: 60s, fully supported. Sided. Held stretch, not super_sweaty eligible.

items.dose: No Sweat: 60s per side, settled, breath-led
items.tierNote: Gentle thoracic/shoulder release to begin down-regulation. No Sweat: 60s settled per side. Sided. Winddown-eligible.

items.dose: No Sweat: 75s, long slow breaths, 1 round
items.tierNote: Restorative spinal decompression; this IS the rest, no separate rest needed. No Sweat: long 75s hold with slow breathing as the body settles.

items.dose: No Sweat: 90s of pure rest, 1 round
items.tierNote: Primary restorative down-regulation. No Sweat: 90s of pure rest, legs supported up a wall or on a chair. This is the rest — no further rest needed.

items.dose: No Sweat: 90s, still, slow breaths between affirmations, 1 round
items.tierNote: Mandatory affirmation close. No Sweat sits at the longer end (90s) for a generous settling. Pairs the breath with body-neutral, effort-celebrating affirmations and grows the garden +1 — consistency, never intensity.

disclaimers: This session is general movement guidance, not medical advice. It does not diagnose, treat, or replace care from a qualified clinician.

Standard postpartum disclaimer (No Sweat tier): if you have not yet had your postnatal check, or you experience pain, bleeding, dizziness, a feeling of heaviness or bulging in the pelvis, leaking, or a noticeable doming/coning along the midline of your abdomen, stop and consult your provider or a pelvic health physiotherapist before continuing. [evidence: ACOG postpartum exercise guidance recommends individualised clearance and a gradual return; pelvic health physiotherapy consensus supports early breath-led core and pelvic-floor work].
Move within a comfortable range. 'Gentle alternative' cues and the chair/floor path below exist so you can scale any movement down without leaving the session.
Honest guidance only: the 'why' and cues describe how to move, not a clinical prescription. If something does not feel right for your body, skip it — every move is skippable.
screeningGates: No Sweat carries the STANDARD postpartum disclaimer only — no Super Sweaty gate is required. This tier is designed as a safe first option in the return to movement.

Soft self-screen before starting (informational, not a hard block): if you notice pelvic heaviness/bulging, urinary or fecal leaking, or visible abdominal doming during any move, regress to the gentle alternative or stop and seek a pelvic health physiotherapist. [evidence: pelvic health physiotherapy consensus].
This session contains NO crunches, sit-ups, full floor planks, jumping, or impact, consistent with the diastasis-recti-safe hard rules for the postpartum flagship.
The Super Sweaty screening gate (12+ weeks postpartum, provider-cleared, no red-flag symptoms, no leaking/heaviness/doming) is NOT invoked here because no super_sweaty movements are present.
sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion: the avatar honors the existing reduced-motion CSS/JS; with motion reduced (or if WebGL/pose data is unavailable) every move falls back to its aria-live captions and spoken cues, and no shape requires watching the avatar to follow along. Chair/floor alternative path — the whole session can be done without getting onto the floor: arrive (pelvic-breath) seated in a chair; neck-rolls / shoulder-rolls / cat-cow (seated cat-cow, hands on thighs) / hip-circles (seated or standing with chair support) / spinal-wave-standing (seated roll-down over the thighs); pelvic-floor-connect seated; bridge -> seated or standing glute squeeze, or bridge on a bed if floor is hard; low-lunge -> seated figure-four-style hip opener or a supported standing lunge with chair; bird-dog -> seated opposite arm/leg reach, or hands on a counter; seated-twist seated as written; clamshell -> seated knee-opens against light hand resistance; figure-four -> seated ankle-over-knee; thread-needle -> seated cross-body reach; childs-pose -> seated forward fold over a cushion on the thighs; legs-up -> recline with calves on a chair seat, or seated with feet elevated; kind-close seated. Every move is skippable, captions are paired to every cue, and the safety modal is focus-trapped.

requiredFixes: MINOR (copy-finalization, not clinical): the pelvic-floor-connect 'breath' string is truncated mid-sentence ('this teaches both contraction and') — finalize the cue text when the move id/cues are authored as part of the Definition-of-Done.

DoD tracking (not a session defect): when appending the two approved-new ids, build spinal-wave-standing's new keyframe rig OR ship its documented caption-only fallback, and wire pelvic-floor-connect to reuse the pelvic-breath rig as planned; append both ids to the library so id-resolution stays consistent.

sessions.intent: A moderate, talk-test "working but conversational" 30-minute session for postpartum women aged 30-40. It opens with a seated breath to arrive and re-establish the breath-core connection, raises temperature through dynamic mobility and one light low-impact cardio primer, then moves through a breath-led, diastasis-recti-safe main block that alternates posterior-chain and lower-body strength with mobility/cardio and a single short cardio burst. It deliberately avoids crunches, sit-ups, floor planks, and any jumping or impact, and closes with a full down-regulating wind-down and a kind affirmation breath. The goal is consistency and showing up, never intensity or appearance.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome back. There is nowhere else you need to be right now. Let us begin seated, soften the shoulders, and find the breath together. You are exactly where you need to be.
sessions.veraClose: That was steady, honest work, and you showed up for yourself today. However today felt, it counts. Carry one slow breath with you, and one kind thought. You are doing beautifully.

items.tierNote: Seated arrival breath required by session shape. Pelvic-breath is slightly_sweaty eligible and doubles as the postpartum core-connection anchor (breath before load), reconnecting the diaphragm-pelvic-floor system before any strength work.

items.tierNote: slightly_sweaty low-impact cardio woven between strength to keep heart rate gently up; conversational talk-test pace, no impact.

items.tierNote: slightly_sweaty deep-core/anti-rotation strength; the DR-safe substitute for crunches/planks, breath-led with exhale on reach.

items.tierNote: slightly_sweaty posterior upper-body work to counter postpartum feeding/carrying posture; soft-vs-strength balance against the cardio bursts.

items.tierNote: slightly_sweaty held glute/hip release; mandatory down-regulating wind-down begins, lowering arousal after the cardio bursts.

items.tierNote: slightly_sweaty gentle spinal decompression; not a Super Sweaty pace, used as a slow exhale-led release.

disclaimers: This session is general movement guidance, not medical advice, and is not a substitute for assessment by a qualified clinician.

Postpartum return-to-exercise should be individualized. Obtain clearance from your maternity care provider or a pelvic health physiotherapist before progressing, especially after a caesarean birth or any complication (aligned with ACOG postpartum exercise guidance, which advises gradual, individualized return and clearance where concerns exist).

Stop and consult a pelvic health physiotherapist if you notice urinary or faecal leakage, a feeling of vaginal heaviness or bulging, abdominal doming or coning along the midline, or pain. These can indicate the load is too high for now (pelvic health physiotherapy consensus).
Keep effort to a conversational talk-test level for this Slightly Sweaty tier; if you cannot speak in short sentences, ease back (ACSM moderate-intensity guidance).
Breathe out on the effort of each strength repetition and avoid breath-holding/straining to help manage intra-abdominal pressure.
All strength work here is diastasis-recti-safe by design: no crunches, sit-ups, floor planks, or jumping/impact.
screeningGates: Standard postpartum readiness: you feel well, have no active bleeding beyond normal lochia, and have begun gentle activity comfortably. If unsure, treat the No Sweat tier as the first safe option and seek clearance before this moderate tier.

Provider clearance is recommended before moderate (Slightly Sweaty) exercise, particularly within the early postpartum weeks or after caesarean/complicated birth (ACOG: individualized, gradual return).

Symptom gate (carried at this gentler tier as a precaution, not a hard block): if any leaking, heaviness/bulging, or abdominal doming appears during a movement, skip that movement and drop to No Sweat or seek pelvic health physiotherapy assessment.

Note: the dedicated Super Sweaty screening gate (12+ weeks, provider-cleared, no red flags, no leaking/heaviness/doming) is NOT required for this session because no super_sweaty movements were selected; it MUST be enforced for any Super Sweaty session.
sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion: the avatar honors the existing reduced-motion CSS/JS and falls back to aria-live captions; every move is skippable and self-paced, so the light cardio (marching, step-touch, knee-drives) can be slowed to a stroll or replaced with the seated breath beat without losing the session. Chair/floor alternative path: standing strength and cardio can be done seated or holding a sturdy chair/counter for balance (squats become sit-to-stand from the chair; wall-pushup stays at the wall; standing-row done seated; marching/step-touch/knee-drives done seated as alternating heel taps and arm pulls). Floor wind-down moves (figure-four, reclined-twist) can be done seated in a chair with an ankle crossed over the opposite knee for figure-four and a gentle seated rotation for the twist. The arrival breath and kind-close are already seated and unchanged.

requiredFixes: Minor (polish, non-blocking): the second disclaimer cites 'ACOG postpartum exercise guidance' descriptively with no number/year (the correct conservative choice), but per the no-fabricated-citation rule add an explicit '[evidence: thin - flag]' marker rather than leaving the guidance attribution unqualified.

Track as Definition-of-Done (not a per-session defect): append the six approved-new ids (marching, step-touch, wall-pushup, standing-row, knee-drives, reclined-twist) to js/data/exercises.js with full schema and wire each pose-plan (reuse tree-pose for marching/knee-drives; reuse figure-four supine base for reclined-twist; new stand-base row rig for standing-row; documented caption-only fallback for step-touch and wall-pushup) into js/data/poses.js before this session ships to users.

Track as DoD (out-of-band, matrix prerequisite): implement the postpartum screening data model -- capture weeks-postpartum, caesarean/complication flag, pelvic-floor red-flag self-screen -- and bump the store in js/state.js to v2 with a migrate() case (scaffold exists at js/state.js:35); enforce the declared gates app-side and route flagged users to No Sweat/Meditation + clinician prompt.

sessions.intent: A vigorous-within-the-postpartum-ceiling 30-minute session that raises and sustains heart rate through low-impact cardio intervals supersetted with deep-core and posterior-chain strength, then deliberately and fully down-regulates. NEVER crunches, sit-ups, floor planks, or jumping. Core load is restricted to diastasis-recti-safe patterns (bridge, bird-dog with knee hover, supported dead bug) and incline rather than floor anti-extension work. This session is gated: it is only appropriate for someone 12+ weeks postpartum, cleared by their provider, with no red flags (no bleeding, pain, leaking, heaviness, or abdominal doming). The mandatory longer wind-down is part of the dose, not optional.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome. You showed up, and that already counts. Let us begin seated and unhurried — soften the jaw, let the shoulders drop, and find a slow breath. There is real work ahead today, and we will meet it at a strong, steady pace that still respects your body. Nothing here is about pushing through pain. If anything feels like leaking, heaviness, or a doming down the middle, you ease off, and that is wisdom, not weakness. Let us build some heat together, {name}.
sessions.veraClose: That was strong, sustained work, and you saw it through. Notice the warmth in your body and the slowing of your breath — this stillness is the other half of the effort. You did not have to earn this rest; it was always yours. You are exactly where you need to be, {name}. Come back whenever you are ready — the garden grows from showing up, never from how hard you went.

items.dose: 90s, metered 4-4-4-4
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty arrival. Box-breath is tier-eligible for warmup/close; used here as the mandatory seated arrival breath to set a calm baseline before vigorous work and to consciously down-regulate the nervous system before loading. Kept at 90s (toward the longer end) so heart rate and breath start from a settled place.

items.dose: 50s, bigger brisker circles
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty eligible (gated). Larger, brisker circles to raise shoulder-girdle temperature before loaded upper-body work (wall push-up, standing row).

items.dose: 55s, larger brisker circles, switch direction at halfway
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty eligible (gated). Larger, brisker circles as a lower-body dynamic warm-up before squats and crescent lunge.

items.dose: 55s, fuller brisker rotations
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty eligible (gated). Fuller, brisker spinal warm-up to prepare rotational tolerance before the main block. Smooth and controlled, no end-range snapping.

items.dose: 45s per side (90s total), ~10-12 controlled swings each, switch at halfway
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty eligible (gated), sided. Smooth rhythmic swings, no momentum-bouncing at end range, to open the hips before cardio intervals and lunges.

items.dose: 60s, rhythmic low-impact side steps ~1/sec, add arm sweeps
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty eligible (gated), cardio. Begins gently raising heart rate and grooves the lateral-step pattern used later in skater-steps. Continuous, no rest within the set, low-impact (no jumping).

items.dose: 45s quicker tempo x 2 rounds, ~20s rest between
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty cardio (gated). Low-impact step-out jacks (step out, not jump) to bring heart rate into the working zone. Opens the cardio-strength superset rhythm of the main block.

items.dose: 12-15 reps x 3 sets, 2s pause at the bottom, rest 20s
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty (gated) strength. Invisible-chair squats as the primary lower-body strength driver, supersetted against the preceding cardio. Controlled tempo, neutral spine, exhale on the rise to manage intra-abdominal pressure (no breath-holding/bearing down).

items.dose: 45s quicker with a deeper reach x 2 rounds, 30s rest between
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty cardio (gated). Lateral low-impact intervals (step, never bound/jump). Second cardio block to sustain heart rate while shifting to a frontal-plane pattern.

items.dose: 5-8 reps per side x 3 sets, 2-3s hover hold, controlled, rest 30-45s
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty ONLY, sided, gated. Diastasis-recti-safe anti-rotation/anti-extension core progression — replaces any crunch/plank work. Requires the Super Sweaty screening gate; deliberately not offered below top tier. Stop and regress to standard bird-dog if any abdominal doming appears.

items.dose: 45s per side at a quicker rhythmic tempo, 20s rest between sides
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty cardio (gated), sided. Standing knee drives keep one foot grounded (low-impact, no jumping) while driving heart rate; pairs with the upcoming posterior-chain strength.

items.dose: 12-15 reps x 3 sets with single-leg march at top, slow 3s eccentric lower, rest 30s
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty (gated) strength. Glute/posterior-chain and deep-core driver; single-leg march at top adds an anti-rotation core demand that is diastasis-safe. Exhale and gently engage the deep core on the lift; no breath-holding.

items.dose: 45s faster combinations
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty cardio (gated). Upper-body-led interval to sustain heart rate and add a fun, rhythmic burst; feet stay grounded and soft (no jumping).

items.dose: incline reps at counter height 10-12 x 3 sets, lowering for 3s, rest 20s
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty (gated) strength. Incline push-up at counter height for the upper-body pushing pattern — an INCLINE press, never a floor plank/push-up, keeping load off the midline. Slow 3s eccentric for control.

items.dose: 4-6 slow pulses then hold, 45s per side, arms up, 6s transition between sides
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty (gated) yoga/strength, sided. Single-leg strength and balance with a heart-rate component from the pulses; a strong, controlled close to the working block before the engine alternates back to wind-down. Keep ribs stacked over pelvis to avoid flaring/doming.

items.dose: 50s per side, settled hold, 6s transition between sides
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty wind-down begins the mandatory longer down-regulation. Releases the hip flexors heavily loaded by lunges, squats, and knee drives. Slow, breath-led, back knee padded as needed.

items.dose: 50-70s per side, still hold after a slow entry, a breath between sides
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty eligible, sided. Spine-decompressing closer after rotational and posterior-chain work; gentle, supported, breath-led.

items.dose: 90-120s, pure rest
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty eligible. The single best down-regulation closer after vigorous work — restorative, drops heart rate, settles the pelvic floor. Held toward the longer end because the longer wind-down is mandatory at this tier.

items.dose: 120s, in for 4 / out for 6-8
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty eligible breath. Exhale-led long slow breath to actively bring heart rate and breath down before the close; bridges the restorative rest into the affirmation close.

items.dose: 90s, slow breaths between affirmations
items.tierNote: Tier-eligible close for all tiers. Final kind-thoughts affirmation; 90s (the non-meditation length) seals the down-regulation and records the breath-close for progression.

disclaimers: This session is general movement guidance, not medical advice. It does not diagnose, treat, or replace care from a qualified clinician.

Super Sweaty is the most demanding tier. It is appropriate only if you are at least 12 weeks postpartum and have been cleared to exercise by your healthcare provider. If you have not been cleared, choose No Sweat or Slightly Sweaty, or a Meditation, instead.

Stop and seek guidance from your provider or a pelvic-health physiotherapist if you notice any urine or stool leaking, a feeling of vaginal heaviness or bulging, pelvic or abdominal pain, a visible doming or coning down the midline of your belly, dizziness, or any vaginal bleeding.
Breathe throughout every effort. Exhale on the hardest part of each movement and do not hold your breath or bear down, which raises pressure on the pelvic floor and abdominal wall.

All movements here are low-impact by design. There is no jumping, and no crunches, sit-ups, or floor planks, in line with diastasis-recti-safe practice. Modify or skip anything that does not feel right today. [evidence: ACOG postpartum exercise guidance supports a gradual return after clearance; pelvic-health physiotherapy consensus supports avoiding breath-holding/Valsalva and high-pressure loading until symptom-free. Specific week/symptom thresholds simplified for a consumer audience — flag for clinical review.]
screeningGates: SUPER SWEATY GATE (all must be true): at least 12 weeks postpartum.
SUPER SWEATY GATE: cleared to exercise by your healthcare provider (or pelvic-health physiotherapist).
SUPER SWEATY GATE: no red-flag symptoms today — no urine or stool leaking, no vaginal heaviness or bulging, no pelvic or abdominal pain, no abdominal doming/coning, no vaginal bleeding.

If any gate fails, this session is withheld and the user is routed to a gentler tier (No Sweat or Slightly Sweaty) or a Meditation, each carrying the standard postpartum disclaimer that exercise should follow provider clearance.
Standard postpartum disclaimer (carried by all tiers): movement is general guidance, not medical advice; resume or progress exercise in line with your provider's clearance and stop on any symptom.
sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion: the avatar honors the OS reduced-motion setting and falls back to aria-live captions paired to every cue; no movement requires watching the screen. Chair/floor alternative path: every standing item has a seated or supported swap — squats become sit-to-stand from a sturdy chair; crescent and the cardio intervals (step-jacks, skater-steps, knee-drives, shadow-box) become seated marching, seated arm punches, and seated heel/toe taps at your own brisk pace; wall push-up can be done seated pressing into a counter or sofa-back. Floor work (bird-dog knee hover, bridge, reclined twist, legs-up, low-lunge) can be done on a bed or padded surface; bird-dog knee hover can stay a standard knee-down bird-dog, and legs-up can be done with calves resting on a chair seat. Every move is skippable, and the arrival breath and kind close require no movement at all.

requiredFixes: MINOR/non-blocking: extended-exhale-breath in winddown is typed 'breath' and dosed 120s, exceeding the stated 'breath item <= 90s' guardrail. It is clinically defensible (no-hold, exhale-led restorative down-regulation where the long exhale is the active ingredient), but to satisfy the literal cap either trim to <=90s or reclassify it as a winddown/restorative (non-breath) item so the breath-cap rule does not apply.

sessions.intent: A long, restorative No Sweat practice for postpartum bodies: reconnect breath to deep core and pelvic floor, move every major joint through gentle range, and rebuild foundational glute, back, and standing strength with held positions, slow tempo, and generous rest so the heart rate barely lifts. Pace is breath-led throughout; this is the first safe option in the early return to movement and a calm reset on a tired day. Clinical framing follows ACOG postpartum exercise guidance (gradual, symptom-guided return) and pelvic-health physiotherapy consensus on breath-led deep-core and pelvic-floor reconnection (transversus abdominis and pelvic floor coordinated with the breath). Core work is diastasis-recti-safe by design: no crunches, sit-ups, or floor planks, no impact, no jumping.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome. Come to a comfortable seat and let your shoulders melt down. There is nowhere else to be for the next little while. We will start with the breath, move slowly, and rest often. You are exactly where you need to be, {name}.
sessions.veraClose: That was time you gave to yourself, and it counts. However today felt, you showed up and your body thanks you for it. Carry this softness with you. You are doing beautifully, {name}.

items.tierNote: No Sweat dose: 40s, smaller range. blocks:warmup, no_sweat eligible. Gentle cervical wake-up with no rest needed.

items.dose: 45s — small, lazy circles, ~5s each, both directions.

disclaimers: This session is general movement guidance, not medical advice, and does not replace care from your doctor, midwife, or a pelvic-health physiotherapist.

Begin gentle postpartum movement only once you feel ready; ACOG guidance supports a gradual, symptom-guided return after an uncomplicated birth, and many providers review activity around the 6-week postpartum check. Your own timeline may differ, especially after a caesarean or a complicated birth.

Stop and consult a professional if you notice any of the following during or after movement: pelvic or vaginal heaviness or bulging, urinary or faecal leaking, a visible doming or coning down the midline of your abdomen, increased or bright-red bleeding, pain, or dizziness. These can be signs the load is too much for now.

All cues are honest movement guidance only and are never a diagnosis or treatment. If you have diastasis recti, prolapse symptoms, pelvic pain, or any health condition, seek individualised assessment.
Move at your own pace. Every movement is optional and skippable, and rest is always allowed.
screeningGates: Standard postpartum disclaimer applies (this is the No Sweat tier): confirm you feel ready to begin gentle movement and that you have no untreated postpartum red flags. No provider-clearance gate is required to start this gentle, low-load, no-impact tier, though early postpartum it is wise to have had your provider's go-ahead.

If at any point you experience pelvic heaviness or bulging, leaking, abdominal doming/coning, pain, dizziness, or increased bleeding, stop the session and seek advice before continuing — these symptoms gate progression to any higher tier.

This No Sweat session deliberately includes NO Super Sweaty content, so the Super Sweaty screening gate (12+ weeks postpartum, provider-cleared, no red flags, no leaking/heaviness/doming) is not engaged here. That gate is required only before any Super Sweaty tier session.
sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion: this is already a slow, low-amplitude practice; if you use reduced-motion settings, the avatar animation simplifies and every cue is mirrored in aria-live captions, so you can follow by reading alone. Chair/floor alternative path: the whole session can be done without getting onto the floor. Standing/seated moves (neck-rolls, shoulder-rolls, arm-sweeps, side-reach, spinal-wave-standing, hip-circles, chair-pose) stay as-is. For the floor-based moves, use these seated/standing/supported swaps — bridge and glute-bridge-march: seated or standing glute squeezes, or a supported bridge on a firm sofa; clamshell, side-lying-leg-lift: seated banded knee press or standing hip abduction holding a chair; bird-dog: standing bird-dog hinged at a table, opposite arm and leg reaching; kickbacks: standing kickback holding a chair; down-dog and cobra-childs-flow: wall down-dog and gentle standing back-bend with hands on the low back; low-lunge, figure-four, butterfly: seated figure-four and seated butterfly in a chair; thread-needle, half-pigeon, reclined-twist, seated-twist, cat-cow: seated thread-the-needle and seated twists, cat-cow on hands at a table; legs-up: recline with calves on a chair seat, or simply sit with feet elevated. Keep a sturdy chair and a wall within reach. All breath work (pelvic-breath, pelvic-floor-connect, kind-close) is done seated and needs no adaptation.

requiredFixes: Minor (non-blocking): pelvic-breath (120s) and kind-close (120s) exceed the 90s breath-item soft band cited in the gate criterion (content-spec generation default is 60-90s for breath/close). They are intentional restorative arrival-anchor and affirmation-close holds appropriate to a 45-min restorative practice, and the actual deep-core breath PRIMER (pelvic-floor-connect) is correctly held at 60s. Optionally annotate these two as deliberate long-end restorative holds, or trim toward ~90s, to keep the band claim self-consistent. Not a load or safety defect.

sessions.intent: A moderate, low-impact 45-minute postpartum session that warms the whole body, builds deep-core and glute strength with unilateral work, adds gentle low-impact cardio at a conversational "talk-test" pace, and down-regulates fully before a kind close. Working but never breathless; no jumping, no impact, no crunches/sit-ups/floor planks. Aligned with ACOG guidance that postpartum people who feel ready may gradually resume moderate-intensity aerobic and strength activity, progressing as comfort allows.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome in. Let us begin seated, with one slow breath. There is nowhere to rush to and nothing to prove — your only job for the next while is to move with yourself, kindly. Settle your weight, soften your jaw, and let this first breath arrive all the way down. You are exactly where you need to be.
sessions.veraClose: That is your practice complete. You showed up for your body today, and that is the whole win — the garden grows because you returned, never because you pushed. Carry this steadiness with you. You are strong, you are capable, and you did this for yourself.

items.dose: 6-8 slow breath cycles, ~4s inhale / 6s exhale, seated; ~50s
items.tierNote: Seated arrival breath is mandatory in every session. Pelvic-breath doubles as the postpartum deep-core connection anchor, priming breath-led core engagement before any loading. Restorative, not exertional — correct as the settle-in regardless of tier.

items.dose: 50s, bigger brisker circles, ~5s per slow circle
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty takes the fuller, brisker range to raise shoulder-girdle temperature before the wall-incline-press loaded upper-body work later.

items.dose: 50s, generous circles ~6s each, switching direction at the halfway cue
items.tierNote: Lower-body dynamic warm-up. Moderate range for Slightly Sweaty — larger than No Sweat but unhurried, prepping hips for squats and lunges.

items.dose: 50s, smooth rhythmic rotations side to side, ~4s per full pass
items.tierNote: Spinal warm-up at a moderate, controlled tempo. Gentle rotation only — no forced range, appropriate for a recovering trunk.

items.dose: 45s per side (~90s total), 10-12 controlled swings each side; switch at the halfway cue
items.tierNote: Sided. Slightly Sweaty eligible. Smooth and rhythmic with no end-range bouncing — opens the hips and warms the legs before cardio and unilateral strength.

items.dose: 55s rhythmic low-impact side step-outs, ~1 step/sec, with optional arm sweeps; continuous, no rest
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty eligible. Light low-impact cardio prep to gently lift the heart rate ahead of the main block. No jumping; weight stays grounded each step.

items.dose: 8 slides per side (~90s total), slide out 3s / return 3s, brief pause at full extension
items.tierNote: Sided. In Slightly Sweaty, heel-slides serve as a low-load deep-core connection primer (per its tier note it is a warm-up primer at higher tiers), reinforcing breath-with-movement before loaded core work like deadbug and bird-dog. Diastasis-recti-safe.

items.dose: 10-12 reps x 2 sets, 2s up / 2s hold / 2s down, rest 30s
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty dose. DR-safe glute/posterior-chain strength led by exhale-on-lift; a foundational postpartum core-and-glute move. Leads the main block as the engine alternates strength first.

items.dose: 60s standing march, ~110-120 steps/min, 1 set, 20s rest into next move
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty low-impact cardio burst. Conversational 'talk-test' pace — working but able to speak. No impact, feet stay low.

items.dose: 12-15 reps per side x 2 sets (~ per side), open 2s / close 2s, rest 30s
items.tierNote: Sided. Slightly Sweaty dose. Unilateral glute-medius strength supporting pelvic stability — key postpartum hip work. Alternates in as strength after the cardio burst.

items.dose: 2 holds per side, 30-40s each, rest 25s
items.tierNote: Sided. Slightly Sweaty dose. Steady standing lower-body and shoulder endurance hold; builds leg work without impact. Provides the mobility/yoga counterpoint to the strength reps.

items.dose: 8 reps per side x 2 sets, 2s reach / 2s hold / 2s return
items.tierNote: Sided. Slightly Sweaty dose. The cornerstone DR-safe anti-rotation core move — quadruped, breath-led, no spinal flexion. Replaces any crunch/plank for trunk work.

items.dose: 60s continuous, ~1 step-touch/sec, 1 set, 15-20s rest
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty low-impact cardio. Keeps the heart rate in a moderate conversational range mid-block. No jumping; one foot stays grounded.

items.dose: 10-12 reps x 2 sets, tempo controlled, rest 25s
items.tierNote: Invisible-chair squats at Slightly Sweaty dose. Bilateral functional lower-body strength; depth to comfort, knees tracking toes, exhale on the rise.

items.dose: 50s per side, slow forward-and-back rock
items.tierNote: Sided. Slightly Sweaty dose. Hip-flexor and quad opener that counters the squat/lunge loading; mobility breather within the alternation.

items.dose: 6-8 reps per side, lower 3s / return 3s, rest 30s
items.tierNote: Sided. Slightly Sweaty eligible. ALWAYS feet-supported — never the full unsupported double-leg lower. Breath-led anti-extension deep-core control, DR-safe; no doming permitted.

items.dose: 40s per side, controlled, 1 set, 20s rest between sides
items.tierNote: Sided. Slightly Sweaty cardio at a controlled tempo — a light low-impact burst that also challenges single-leg stability. No hopping; drive the knee, plant the foot softly.

items.dose: 3 holds of 20-30s at a wall or counter incline, rest 30s
items.tierNote: INCLINE hold against a raised surface — never a floor plank, per the postpartum safety rule. Slightly Sweaty dose builds shoulder/trunk endurance with low intra-abdominal pressure.

items.dose: 50s, a few rounds linking strongly to breath, ~5-6s per wave
items.tierNote: Closes the main block as a gentle spinal mobiliser, beginning the transition toward wind-down. Slow breath-led waves decompress the spine after standing and quadruped work.

items.dose: 50s per side, settled hold
items.tierNote: Sided. Slightly Sweaty dose (held stretch, not super_sweaty-eligible). Releases the glutes and outer hips after clamshells, lunges and cardio.

items.dose: 45s per side, breath-led — twist a little further on each exhale
items.tierNote: Sided. Slightly Sweaty dose. Gentle spinal decompression; gentle range only, no forcing through a recovering trunk.

items.dose: 45-60s, slow gentle rocking or still
items.tierNote: Reclined hip-release down-regulation. Soothes the lower back and inner thighs and shifts the nervous system toward rest before the close.

items.dose: 75-90s, in for 4 / out for 6-8, exhale-led
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty dose. Exhale-led breathing actively brings heart rate and breath down after the moderate work — the mandatory down-regulation bridge into the close.

items.dose: 75-90s, still, slow breaths between affirmations
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty dose. The final affirmation close — body-neutral, celebrating showing up. Mandatory in every session; this completion grows the garden +1 exactly like any other.

disclaimers: This session is general movement guidance, not medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. It does not replace care from your doctor, midwife, or pelvic health physiotherapist.

Standard postpartum disclaimer (Slightly Sweaty tier): before resuming or progressing exercise after birth, confirm with your healthcare provider that you are cleared to do so. ACOG guidance is that activity may be resumed gradually when you feel ready and have no complications; recovery timelines differ for vaginal birth, cesarean birth, and any complications.

Stop and consult a provider if you notice any abdominal doming or coning, pelvic or vaginal heaviness or bulging, urinary or fecal leaking, vaginal bleeding that restarts or increases, pain, dizziness, or shortness of breath beyond a conversational 'talk-test' effort.

This session deliberately avoids crunches, sit-ups, floor planks, and any jumping or impact, in line with diastasis-recti-safe and pelvic-floor-protective practice. Keep the Supported Dead Bug feet-supported at all times; never progress to an unsupported double-leg lower here.
All loads, holds, and rep counts are starting suggestions. Reduce range, reps, or tempo at any point. Every movement is skippable with no penalty — consistency, never intensity.
screeningGates: Standard postpartum readiness: you have had your postpartum check and your provider has cleared you for moderate-intensity exercise, OR you are confident you are recovering without complications. If you have not been cleared, choose the No Sweat tier or a Meditation session instead.

Symptom self-check before starting: no abdominal doming/coning, no pelvic heaviness or bulging, no leaking, and no bleeding that has restarted. If any are present, step down to No Sweat or seek pelvic health physiotherapy guidance before moderate work.

Note: this Slightly Sweaty session does NOT require the Super Sweaty screening gate (12+ weeks, provider-cleared, no red flags). No screened-only movements (e.g. bird-dog-knee-hover, fast-feet, crescent-lunge) are included; all cardio here is low-impact at a conversational pace.
sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion: the avatar respects the app's reduced-motion setting and falls back to aria-live captions paired to every cue; if WebGL or pose data is unavailable, the session runs caption-only with no loss of guidance. Chair/floor alternative path: Standing March, Step-Touch, Knee Drives, Squats, Warrior II and the wall press can all be performed seated or with a steady chair/counter for support — march and step in place while seated, do seated mini-squats as sit-to-stand prep, and brace the chair-back for balance. Floor moves (Bridge, Bird Dog, Clamshell, Supported Dead Bug, Figure-Four, Happy Baby, Seated Twist) can be done on a bed or padded surface; Figure-Four and the twist can also be done seated in a chair. Every movement is individually skippable, and rest can be extended at any time.

requiredFixes: Minor sequencing refinement: deadbug-supported (loaded anti-extension deep-core) sits at main position 9 immediately followed by knee-drives (single-leg dynamic trunk cardio) at position 10 — placing the most demanding core-control move plus late single-leg dynamic trunk work when the abdominal wall/pelvic floor is more fatigued. Consider moving knee-drives earlier (e.g. swap with step-touch) or inserting an explicit recovery beat between deadbug-supported and knee-drives so dynamic single-leg trunk work is not the last loaded core demand. Knee-drives is feet-planted/controlled (40s/side, no hop), which mitigates but does not eliminate the ordering concern.

Minor cue completeness: the doming/coning + pelvic-floor-pressure/leaking stop-rule is carried at the global disclaimer/screening level but is not echoed in the per-move tierNote of each loaded core/cardio item (bridge, bird-dog, deadbug-supported, marching, step-touch, knee-drives, wall-incline-press, squats). Add an explicit per-move 'stop if you see doming/coning or feel pelvic-floor pressure/leaking' check cue on each loaded core and cardio move for redundancy at the point of effort.

sessions.intent: A vigorous, low-impact 45-minute strength-and-cardio session for the screened postpartum body: dynamic full-body warm-up, then alternating low-impact cardio intervals with unilateral and deep-core strength supersets at the top end of the postpartum-safe ceiling, followed by a deliberately long, mandatory down-regulation wind-down and breath close. Builds heart rate and load while honoring diastasis-recti and pelvic-floor safety: no crunches, no sit-ups, no floor planks, no jumping. The longer settle and exhale-led breathing actively bring heart rate and the nervous system back down.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome back. You showed up, and that is the whole thing right there. We are going to work today, and we are going to work smart. Find your feet, let your shoulders drop, and take one slow breath with me before we begin. You are exactly where you need to be, {name}.
sessions.veraClose: That was real work, and you met it. Notice how your breath has settled, how your body feels held by the floor. Nothing about today was about changing how you look. It was about what you can do, and what you can do is plenty. Rest here a moment longer. I am proud of you, {name}.

items.dose: 90 secs; 4-4-4-4 metered breathing, seated and settled
items.tierNote: Every practice opens seated with breath, including Super Sweaty. Box breathing here also primes intra-abdominal pressure control before loaded work, which is the deep-core foundation for postpartum-safe vigorous training.

items.dose: 50 secs; bigger, brisker circles, ~5 sec each
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty eligible (requires the screening gate). Raises shoulder-girdle temperature before loaded upper-body work (wall push-ups, rows in spirit).

items.dose: 55 secs; larger, brisker circles, switch direction at halfway
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty eligible (gated). Dynamic lower-body warm-up to mobilize the hips before squats and lunges.

items.dose: 50 secs; fuller, brisker rotations, ~4 sec per pass
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty eligible (gated). Spinal warm-up; rotational prep done standing and controlled, never a loaded twist, keeping it diastasis-safe.

items.dose: ~45 secs per side; 10-12 controlled swings each side, no end-range bounce, switch at halfway
items.tierNote: Sided. Super Sweaty eligible (gated). Dynamic hip mobility to prepare for crescent lunge, knee drives and skaters; controlled range only, no momentum.

items.dose: 60 secs; rhythmic low-impact side steps, ~1 step/sec, arm sweeps optional, continuous
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty eligible (gated). First low-impact cardio bump to lift heart rate before the main intervals. No impact, no jumping.

items.dose: ~75 secs per side; 3-4 continuous rounds per side at a steady flowing pace, one breath per movement
items.tierNote: Sided. Super Sweaty eligible (gated). Links breath to movement and continues building heart rate as a flowing finisher to the warm-up, bridging into the main block.

items.dose: 45 secs quicker tempo, 2 rounds; 20s rest into next move
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty cardio interval. Low-impact step-out pattern (no true jumping jack) to open the main block and drive heart rate; honors the no-jumping rule.

items.dose: 12-15 reps x 3 sets, 2s pause at the bottom, rest 20s between sets
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty (screened). Primary lower-body strength; controlled tempo with bottom pause builds load while keeping intra-abdominal pressure managed. Exhale on the rise to protect the pelvic floor.

items.dose: 45 secs per side at a quicker rhythmic tempo; 20s rest between sides
items.tierNote: Sided cardio. Super Sweaty interval supersetted after squats; standing, low-impact, builds heart rate without floor core flexion.

items.dose: 45 secs per side; add 4-6 slow 1-2 inch pulses before holding; 6s transition between sides
items.tierNote: Sided. Super Sweaty (gated). Unilateral leg strength and balance under fuller range; pulses raise the leg-work and heart-rate demand.

items.dose: 45 secs quicker with a deeper reach, 2 rounds, 30s rest between rounds
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty cardio interval. Lateral low-impact pattern for power and heart rate; lands softly, no jump phase, keeping impact off the pelvic floor.

items.dose: 12-15 reps x 3 sets, single-leg march at the top optional, slower 3s eccentric, rest 30s
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty (gated). Posterior-chain and deep-core strength; the marching/eccentric variation loads the glutes and TA safely on the floor. Exhale and gently lift the pelvic floor on the press up.

items.dose: 45 secs faster combinations; 20s rest
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty cardio. Upper-body-led interval to keep heart rate up between strength supersets; controlled core, no impact.

items.dose: 10 reps per side x 2-3 sets, 2-3s hold at full reach, optional knee-hover, rest 30s
items.tierNote: Sided. Super Sweaty (gated). The diastasis-safe core anti-rotation/anti-extension driver that replaces crunches and planks. Keep the spine neutral and ribs over pelvis throughout.

items.dose: Incline reps at counter height, 10-12 x 3 sets, lowering for 3s, rest 20s
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty (gated). Upper-body pressing strength at an incline, never a floor plank. The incline keeps pressure off the abdominal wall while still loading the chest, shoulders and triceps.

items.dose: 15-20 reps per side x 2-3 sets, 2s hold at the top, light band above knees optional, rest 30s
items.tierNote: Sided. Super Sweaty (gated). Lateral hip/glute-med strength to close the main block; supports pelvic stability that often needs rebuilding postpartum.

items.dose: ~45 secs per side used as a transition/release into the floor; 6s transition between sides
items.tierNote: Sided. Super Sweaty pacing uses it as a flowing release rather than a long hold, opening hip flexors loaded during lunges and squats as the first step of down-regulation.

items.dose: 50 secs per side; 6s transition between sides
items.tierNote: Sided. Held glute/hip release. Not eligible for Super Sweaty pacing, so it is used here at its slightly_sweaty hold to begin the mandatory down-regulation.

items.dose: ~55 secs per side as a spine-decompressing closer; a breath between sides
items.tierNote: Sided. Super Sweaty eligible as a winddown/close item; gently releases the spine after rotational and loaded work.

items.dose: ~90 secs of stillness, legs supported up the wall
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty: the single best down-regulation closer after vigorous work. Reduces lower-limb venous pooling and shifts the nervous system toward rest.

items.dose: ~100 secs; in for 4, out for 6-8, exhale-led
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty: actively brings heart rate and breath back down via a longer exhale (vagal down-regulation). The mandatory longer settle before the close.

items.dose: 90 secs; slow breaths between affirmations, still
items.tierNote: Universal affirmation close at all tiers. Caps the session with effort-based, body-neutral praise after a demanding session.

disclaimers: This session is general exercise guidance, not medical advice, and does not replace assessment by your doctor or a pelvic health physiotherapist.

Super Sweaty is a vigorous tier. Stop and scale down to a gentler tier if you feel any pelvic heaviness or pressure, urinary or fecal leaking, abdominal doming or coning along the midline, pain, dizziness, or any vaginal bleeding, and seek a provider's input before continuing. [evidence: aligns with ACOG postpartum 'Exercise After Pregnancy' guidance to resume activity gradually and consult a provider, and with pelvic health physiotherapy consensus on symptom-led progression]

Breathe throughout: exhale and gently lift the pelvic floor on the effort phase (the lift, the press, the rise), and never hold your breath against a closed glottis during loaded movements. [evidence: thin — flag; reflects standard pelvic-floor physiotherapy coaching on exhale-on-exertion rather than a single numbered guideline]

Core work here is deliberately diastasis-recti-safe: it uses bird-dog and bridges, with no crunches, no sit-ups and no floor planks, and all cardio is low-impact with no jumping. [evidence: consistent with widely used postpartum return-to-exercise consensus, e.g. the Goom/Donnelly/Brockwell 'Returning to running postnatal' guidelines, which advise against high-impact and unmanaged loaded flexion until symptom-free]
If anything feels like too much today, the No Sweat or Slightly Sweaty version of this duration is a complete, valid session. Choosing a gentler tier is progress, not failure.
screeningGates: Super Sweaty gate (all four required before this session is offered): (1) at least 12 weeks postpartum [evidence: ACOG advises a gradual return and an individualized timeline; the 12-week marker is a conservative common-practice threshold, not a fixed ACOG number — flag], (2) cleared for exercise by your healthcare provider at your postpartum check, (3) no red-flag symptoms (no pelvic pressure or heaviness, no urinary or fecal leaking, no abdominal doming/coning, no pain, no bleeding), (4) a self-reported PAR-Q+/readiness check with no unresolved 'yes' answers. [evidence: PAR-Q+/CSEP is a real validated pre-participation screening tool]
Caesarean or complicated delivery, or any active symptoms: do not select Super Sweaty; route to No Sweat or Slightly Sweaty and seek a pelvic health physiotherapy assessment first.
Gentler tiers (No Sweat, Slightly Sweaty) carry only the standard postpartum disclaimer and do not require the provider-cleared gate, though the same stop-on-symptoms rule applies.
sessions.accessibility: Reduced motion: when the system or profile reduced-motion setting is on, the Three.js avatar is suppressed and every cue is delivered as paired aria-live captions, exactly as the existing player does; no functionality depends on the animation. Chair/floor alternative path: this session can be done largely standing and chair-supported. Squats, hip hinge demand and reverse-lunge load can be replaced by sit-to-stand from a sturdy chair; wall push-ups can move to a higher counter or wall to reduce load; crescent lunge and knee drives can be performed holding a chair back for balance. Floor work (bridge, bird-dog, figure-four, reclined-twist, legs-up) has seated or wall-supported equivalents: bridge becomes seated glute squeezes, bird-dog becomes a seated opposite arm/leg reach with a neutral spine, and the reclined releases become a seated forward fold and a seated gentle twist. Legs-up can be done with calves resting on a chair seat instead of up a wall. Every move remains individually skippable, and the seated arrival breath and the kind close are always retained.

requiredFixes: Sequencing (pelvicFloorDiastasisSafe / prior concern 6): bird-dog, the diastasis-safe deep-core anti-rotation/anti-extension driver, is placed late in the main block after step-jacks, squats, knee-drives, crescent-lunge, skater-steps, bridge and shadow-box — i.e. under peak cardio/strength fatigue. Move bird-dog (and consider a short breath-led core re-set) earlier in the main block, while pressure-management control is fresh, to reduce doming/coning risk. This is a real ordering fix, not an artifact.

Dose cap (cumulativeLoadOK): extended-exhale-breath is dosed at ~100s, exceeding the stated <=90s ceiling for breath items. Trim to <=90s (e.g. ~85-90s) to satisfy the breath-item duration rule; the longer-exhale down-regulation intent is preserved.

Minor declaration polish: the exhale-on-exertion disclaimer is self-marked [evidence: thin — flag] and the 12-week threshold is flagged as common-practice rather than a fixed ACOG number. Keep these honest flags; optionally anchor to a named pelvic-health physiotherapy return-to-impact consensus rather than leaving the exhale cue thin.

sessions.intent: A full hour of gentle, restorative reconnection for the postpartum body. No sweat, no rush: breath-led arrival, an unhurried head-to-toe warm-up, a long main block that re-establishes the deep-core and pelvic-floor connection before layering in low-load gluteal and standing strength holds (alternated with mobility breathers), then a generous wind-down of supported hip and spine releases, finishing with deep down-regulating rest and a kind affirmation. Heart rate barely lifts; the work is reconnection and ease, never intensity. Suitable as a first safe re-entry to movement, including years postpartum.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome. There is nowhere else you need to be for the next hour — just here, with your breath. Settle into a comfortable seat, let your shoulders melt down, and let us begin slowly. You are exactly where you need to be, {name}.
sessions.veraClose: That was a whole hour of showing up for yourself, and it counts every bit as much as any other. Notice how your body feels now — softer, more yours. Carry this ease with you. You did this, {name}.

disclaimers: This session is general movement guidance for wellbeing, not medical advice and not a substitute for care from your doctor or a pelvic health physiotherapist.

Postpartum re-entry should be gradual and individualised. If you have not yet had your postnatal check, or you are early postpartum, consider beginning with only the breath, pelvic-floor connection, and gentle mobility, and add the strength holds when you feel ready.

Stop and consult a professional if you notice pain, dizziness, vaginal bleeding that increases with activity, urinary or faecal leaking, a sensation of heaviness or bulging in the pelvis, or any doming or coning along the midline of your abdomen. These are signs to scale back and seek guidance.
Everything here is low-impact and diastasis-recti-aware: no crunches, no sit-ups, no floor planks, no jumping. Move within a comfortable range and rest whenever you wish — every move is skippable.

[evidence: ACOG postpartum exercise guidance supports a gradual return to low-impact activity and pelvic-floor work; pelvic health physiotherapy consensus supports breath-led deep-core reconnection as a first step. Specific timelines vary by individual and delivery, so no fixed week-count is asserted for this gentle tier.]
screeningGates: Standard postpartum disclaimer applies (this gentle No Sweat tier does NOT require the Super Sweaty screening gate): designed as a safe first re-entry option, but the user is advised to have had, or to be progressing toward, their postnatal check and to stop if any red-flag symptom (pain, increasing bleeding, leaking, pelvic heaviness/bulging, or abdominal doming) appears.

No vigorous, screened-only content is present: no movement in this session requires the 12-weeks / provider-cleared / no-red-flags / no-leaking-or-doming Super Sweaty gate. Should this track ever offer Super Sweaty content, that gate MUST be enforced before those movements are presented.
sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion: honoured automatically — the Three.js avatar respects the existing reduced-motion CSS/JS and degrades to static aria-live captions; several items in this session (wrist-prep, ankle-prep, heel-slides, glute-bridge-march, side-lying-leg-lift, half-pigeon, spinal-wave-standing, pelvic-floor-connect, extended-exhale-breath, cobra-childs-flow) have no dedicated pose rig and run caption-first by design, so a reduced-motion or no-WebGL experience loses nothing. Chair/floor alternative path: the whole session can be done from a sturdy chair or fully on the floor. Standing work (squats, warrior2, tree-pose, goddess, hip-circles, arm-sweeps, spinal-wave-standing) can be done seated or with a hand on a chair/wall for balance — cue this explicitly for tree-pose and warrior2. Floor work (bridge, bird-dog, clamshell, heel-slides, glute-bridge-march, side-lying-leg-lift, figure-four, half-pigeon, thread-needle, low-lunge, cobra-childs-flow) can be padded with a folded blanket; figure-four and seated-twist have seated-in-chair equivalents. The closers (legs-up, supported rest, breath, kind-close) can be done reclined, seated, or with legs along a sofa rather than up a wall. Every move is skippable and the arrival breath plus kind-close are reachable on their own for the lowest-energy day.

requiredFixes: Concern #2 (REAL): add an explicit doming/coning detection CUE to each loaded deep-core move (pelvic-floor-connect, bridge, glute-bridge-march, heel-slides, side-lying-leg-lift), e.g. 'Watch the midline of your belly — if you see a ridge/doming or feel heaviness or leaking, ease off or skip.' Verified gap: existing library cues and the NEW tierNotes assert 'no doming' but never give the user the means to detect it; doming appears only in the global stop-sign disclaimer, not at point-of-performance.

Concern #5 (REAL, minor): add a positional caution for the extended flat-supine time (legs-up 120s + supine main cluster) and the standing roll-down (spinal-wave-standing) — 'come up slowly to avoid light-headedness; exit supine via knees-bent side-roll.' Currently dizziness is only a stop-sign in the disclaimer, with no proactive transition cue.

Concern #3 (REAL, partial): reduce up/down floor transitions in the main block — the floor-strength cluster thrashes supine→quadruped→side-lying→supine→side-lying→quadruped→prone. Group same-position moves (cluster the two supine, then the two side-lying, then quadruped/prone) to lower the get-up/down load and fall/dizziness risk for a first re-entry. The declared chair/floor substitution path is acceptable; this is a grouping refinement, not a missing declaration.

Concern #1 (DECLARATION residual, not a blocker): because the session is marketed as a 'first safe re-entry,' add an explicit DoD note that a real non-dismissible postpartum intake (early-postpartum window/<12wk/C-section/bleeding, pelvic-floor red flags, diastasis self-check, persisted to a store v2 profile and gating/de-loading the loaded-core moves) is required before ship; the current app only has the one-time dismissible seenSafety overlay (state.js:21, main.js:120/153, store version:1). App-side enforcement is DoD per framing, so this does not fail the session, but the re-entry framing should declare the gate as required rather than leaning on a passive disclaimer.

sessions.intent: A full hour for the postpartum body that wants to feel worked but never wrecked. We open by connecting breath to deep core, warm the spine, hips and shoulders thoroughly, then move through a long alternating main block that pairs unilateral strength and yoga-strength holds with short low-impact cardio bursts at a conversational "talk-test" intensity. Core work stays diastasis-recti-safe throughout (breath-led bridges, bird-dog, supported dead bug, incline loading — never crunches, sit-ups, floor planks, or any jumping). A generous wind-down of held releases and a kind-thoughts close bring the nervous system back down. The goal is steady capacity and confidence, not exhaustion: you should finish warm, accomplished, and able to hold a conversation.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome back. You found an hour for yourself today, and that already counts. Come to a comfortable seat. We are going to begin where we always begin — with the breath. Let the belly soften on the way in, and feel a gentle, easy lift through the pelvic floor on the way out. No forcing, no gripping. Just meeting your body exactly where it is right now. You are exactly where you need to be.
sessions.veraClose: That was a full, honest hour, and you stayed with it from the first breath to the last. Let everything soften now. You did not push to empty — you built something, steadily and kindly. Breathe here a moment longer and notice how capable this body is. You showed up for yourself today, and that is enough. You are exactly where you need to be.

disclaimers: This session is general movement guidance for postpartum women and is not medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. It does not replace care from your physician, midwife, or pelvic health physiotherapist.

Postpartum return-to-exercise should follow your own provider's clearance. Major bodies such as ACOG advise that physical activity can typically resume gradually when medically safe and individualised to recovery, including after caesarean birth; your timeline is yours alone. [evidence: ACOG postpartum physical activity guidance — directional; confirm specifics with your provider]

Stop and consult a professional if you notice any of the following during or after movement: vaginal bleeding that increases, pelvic or abdominal pain, a visible bulge or doming along the midline of the abdomen, a feeling of heaviness or dragging at the pelvic floor, or any leaking of urine. These can indicate that the load is too high right now.

This is the Slightly Sweaty tier: you should be working but still able to hold a conversation (the 'talk test'). If you cannot speak in short sentences, ease the pace. [evidence: talk-test as a practical moderate-intensity proxy is consistent with ACSM exertion guidance — directional]

All core work here is selected to be diastasis-recti-aware: there are no crunches, sit-ups, floor planks, or jumping. If any movement provokes doming, pain, or symptoms, skip it and favour the breath-led options.
screeningGates: Standard postpartum disclaimer applies: this Slightly Sweaty session assumes you have had your routine postnatal check and have no ongoing complications; if you are unsure, treat the No Sweat tier as your first safe option and seek provider clearance before progressing.

Self-screen before starting: no increasing bleeding, no pelvic pain, no sense of pelvic-floor heaviness or dragging, no abdominal doming, and no urinary leaking. If any are present, do not run this tier today — choose No Sweat or breath-only work and speak to a pelvic health physiotherapist.

This session contains NO super_sweaty movements, so the Super Sweaty screening gate (12+ weeks postpartum, provider-cleared, no red-flag symptoms, no leaking/heaviness/doming) is not invoked here; that gate is required only for the Super Sweaty tier.
sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion is honoured: the avatar falls back to aria-live captions paired to every cue, and the cardio bursts (step-touch, step-backs, knee-drives, shadow-box, lateral-steps, heel-digs) can be performed at a slow, small range with no travel if motion is uncomfortable. Chair/floor path: any standing item can be done seated or chair-supported — sit-to-stand and squats use a chair already; warrior2, crescent-lunge, tree-pose and lateral-steps can be done holding a chair or wall for balance, or seated with marching/leg lifts as a substitute. Floor-based core and wind-down moves (bridge, bird-dog, supported dead bug, glute-bridge-march, figure-four, half-pigeon, thread-needle, legs-up, reclined-twist) can be done on a bed or padded surface; bird-dog and dead bug can be regressed to a single limb at a time. Every move is skippable, and the arrival breath and kind-thoughts close can be done entirely seated.

sessions.intent: A full 60-minute vigorous session built strictly within the postpartum safe ceiling: a thorough dynamic warm-up; a main block that front-loads the diastasis-safe deep-core driver (bird-dog knee-hover) while motor control is fresh, then pairs screened strength progressions with low-impact cardio intervals (no jumping, no impact) and inserts a breath-led recovery beat before the loaded incline isometric so it is never entered under fresh cardio fatigue; followed by a deliberately long, mandatory down-regulation wind-down and a kind affirmation close. Core work stays diastasis-recti-safe throughout (bird-dog hover, incline press, glute-dominant work; never crunches, sit-ups, or floor planks). Gated behind the Super Sweaty screening; if any gate fails, the user is routed to the Slightly Sweaty or No Sweat 60-minute equivalent.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome back. You have set aside a full hour for yourself today, and that already matters. We will build some real heat in the middle, then take our time coming all the way back down. Settle into a comfortable seat, let your shoulders drop, and let us begin with the breath. You are exactly where you need to be.
sessions.veraClose: That was a strong, generous hour of work, and you met it. Notice how your body feels now: warmer, steadier, more yours. Nothing about today was about changing how you look. It was about showing up, and you did. The garden grows the same whether the session was gentle or fierce, because consistency is what makes it bloom. Rest well.

items.dose: 80s, 360-degree ribcage breath, gentle exhale-led pelvic-floor lift, no holds
items.tierNote: Mandatory seated arrival breath; never skipped at any tier. Pelvic-breath is the postpartum-preferred arrival (no breath-holds) and establishes the diaphragm-to-pelvic-floor coordination that is the foundation of all later loaded work: inhale to soften and widen the ribs, exhale to gently lift the floor of the core. Capped within the 90s ceiling.

items.dose: 60s, bigger brisker circles both directions
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty dose (gated): larger, warmer circles to raise shoulder-girdle temperature before loaded upper-body work (wall-incline-press, shadow-box).

items.dose: 65s, larger brisker circles, switch direction at halfway
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty dose (gated): a fuller, brisker lower-body dynamic warm-up to prepare hips for squats, lunges and lateral cardio.

items.dose: 65s, fuller brisker rotations side to side
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty dose (gated): a fuller spinal warm-up. Rotations stay rhythmic and controlled; this is mobility, not loaded rotation, so it remains diastasis-safe.

items.dose: 60s per side (120s total), ~12-14 controlled swings each side, switch at halfway cue
items.tierNote: Slightly/Super Sweaty mobility primer for the hips and hamstrings before lunges and skater work. Smooth and rhythmic, no momentum-bouncing at end range.

items.dose: 190s, 4 continuous rounds at a steady flowing pace
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty dose (gated): the final warm-up bridge. Continuous flowing rounds gently lift the heart rate while linking breath to movement, transitioning from mobility into the working main block. Lengthened by adding rounds (density), not impact.

items.dose: 70s, rhythmic low-impact side steps ~1/sec with arm sweeps
items.tierNote: Slightly/Super Sweaty low-impact cardio warm-up. Pulse-raiser with zero impact (one foot stays grounded), priming the cardiovascular system before the first true interval.

items.dose: Super Sweaty only (screened): 8-10 reps per side x 3 sets, 3s hover hold, rest 30s (~290s)
items.tierNote: FRONT-LOADED first in the main block, on purpose, so this screened deep-core progression is trained while motor control is freshest and before any cardio fatigue accumulates. A quadruped anti-rotation/anti-extension drill that is the diastasis-SAFE substitute for the planks and crunches this tier forbids. Cue a long exhale and a gently lifted floor of the core throughout. If you see your belly dome along the midline, or feel heaviness or leaking, slow down or skip; that is information, not failure. Requires the Super Sweaty screening gate.

items.dose: Super Sweaty (screened): 12-15 reps x 4 sets, 2s pause at the bottom, rest 20s (~300s)
items.tierNote: Lead strength piece after the core driver. Invisible-chair squats are a closed-chain, breath-led lower-body builder; cue exhale-on-effort to manage intra-abdominal pressure and avoid doming. Added a set for density (not impact). Requires the Super Sweaty screening gate.

items.dose: Super Sweaty: 20s fast / 20s recovery march x 3 rounds, then 30s rest (~160s)
items.tierNote: First cardio interval, alternated AFTER strength per the strength/cardio pattern. Light fast feet is low-impact (no jumping), the vigorous heart-rate driver within the postpartum ceiling. If you see your belly dome along the midline, or feel heaviness or leaking, slow down or skip; that is information, not failure. Requires the Super Sweaty screening gate.

items.dose: Super Sweaty (screened): 45s per side with a slow sink-and-rise pulse, rest 20s (~155s)
items.tierNote: Unilateral standing strength and stability. The sink-and-rise pulse adds leg work while staying upright and impact-free. Requires the Super Sweaty screening gate.

items.dose: Super Sweaty: low half-squat each direction, 55s, 20s rest (~95s)
items.tierNote: Second cardio interval. Lateral travelling steps with a half-squat: low-impact, frontal-plane conditioning that the warm-up hip work prepared. If you see your belly dome along the midline, or feel heaviness or leaking, slow down or skip; that is information, not failure. Requires the Super Sweaty screening gate.

items.dose: Super Sweaty (screened): 4-6 slow pulses then hold, 50s per side, rest 20s (~165s)
items.tierNote: Unilateral lunge strength with a controlled pulse to build leg work and heart rate. Arms overhead is optional; keep ribs stacked over pelvis to protect the core and exhale on the effort. Requires the Super Sweaty screening gate.

items.dose: Super Sweaty: 50s faster combinations, 20s rest (~85s)
items.tierNote: Third cardio interval and the upper-body conditioning driver. No impact, all controlled rotation through the trunk; keeps the heart rate up without floor work. If you see your belly dome along the midline, or feel heaviness or leaking, slow down or skip; that is information, not failure. Requires the Super Sweaty screening gate.

items.dose: Super Sweaty (screened): 15-20 reps per side x 3 sets, 2s top hold, optional light band, rest 30s (~265s)
items.tierNote: Glute-medius strength for pelvic and lumbopelvic stability, directly supportive after delivery. Side-lying and impact-free; pairs naturally with the lunge and squat work. Added a set for density (not impact). Requires the Super Sweaty screening gate.

items.dose: Super Sweaty: 50s with a deeper reach, 2 rounds, 30s rest (~130s)
items.tierNote: Fourth and most lateral-dynamic cardio interval. Step-based (no jump), so it stays low-impact while challenging balance and the lateral chain. If you see your belly dome along the midline, or feel heaviness or leaking, slow down or skip; that is information, not failure. Requires the Super Sweaty screening gate.

items.dose: Super Sweaty (screened): 3-5 breaths then step to low-lunge, 2 rounds (~165s)
items.tierNote: RECOVERY BEAT inserted on purpose between the skater-steps cardio interval and the loaded wall-incline isometric, so the sustained anterior-chain hold is never entered under fresh cardio fatigue. Breath-led mobility that decompresses the spine, lengthens the posterior chain, and lets the heart rate settle before the next load. Requires the Super Sweaty screening gate.

items.dose: Super Sweaty (screened): 4 holds of 35-45s at a lower incline with optional slow shoulder taps, rest 30s (~275s)
items.tierNote: Upper-body and trunk strength as an INCLINE hold against a raised surface, explicitly NEVER a floor plank, which this tier forbids for diastasis safety. Entered only after the down-dog recovery beat. The incline reduces intra-abdominal load while still building the anterior chain; cue a steady exhale and watch for doming. Requires the Super Sweaty screening gate.

items.dose: Super Sweaty: ~90s per side as a settled hip-flexor release (~180s total)
items.tierNote: Mandatory down-regulation begins. Hip-flexor lengthening after heavy lunge/squat loading. Used here as a held release rather than a flow transition, easing the body from working to resting; lengthened for density to honor the longer mandatory cool-down.

items.dose: ~87s per side, fully breath-led (~175s total)
items.tierNote: Deep glute and external-rotator release after clamshell and skater work. Not eligible for super_sweaty PACING; correctly placed in the wind-down as a still, restorative hold at its standard supported dose to lengthen the mandatory cool-down.

items.dose: Super Sweaty: 175s, the primary down-regulation rest
items.tierNote: The single best down-regulation closer after vigorous work: legs elevated, fully passive, encouraging venous return and a parasympathetic shift. Honors the rule that the super_sweaty wind-down is mandatory and longer.

items.dose: Super Sweaty: 90s, in for 4 / out for 6-8, exhale-led
items.tierNote: Capped at the 90s ceiling. Actively brings heart rate and breath back down after the session's exertion via lengthened exhales. Placed in wind-down so the unique close remains the affirmation.

items.dose: 90s, still, slow breaths between affirmations
items.tierNote: The single, unique affirmation close (the engine reserves one close move per session), capped at 90s. Body-neutral, effort-celebrating affirmations; never appearance or weight. Counts the session toward the +1 garden growth and the breath-close badge identically to any other tier.

disclaimers: This session is general movement guidance, not medical advice. It does not diagnose, treat, or replace care from your doctor, midwife, or a pelvic-health physiotherapist.

Stop and rest if you feel pain, dizziness, vaginal heaviness or bulging, abdominal coning or doming along the midline, or any urinary or fecal leaking, and seek professional guidance before continuing.
If you have had a cesarean birth, a complicated delivery, ongoing bleeding, or any diagnosed condition, consult your provider before attempting a vigorous (Super Sweaty) session.

Cues and rationale describe honest exercise guidance only; where the evidence base is thin this is flagged rather than overstated, and nothing here is a clinical recommendation for your individual situation.
screeningGates: Super Sweaty gate (ALL required before this session is offered): at least 12 weeks postpartum.
Super Sweaty gate: cleared for vigorous exercise by your healthcare provider (in line with ACOG postpartum return-to-exercise guidance, which advises medical clearance before progressing intensity).
Super Sweaty gate: no red-flag symptoms today, meaning no pain, no vaginal heaviness or bulging, no abdominal doming/coning, no urinary or fecal leaking.
Super Sweaty gate: completed a general pre-exercise readiness check (PAR-Q+/CSEP style); if any item flags risk, route to the Slightly Sweaty or No Sweat 60-minute session instead.
Standard postpartum disclaimer carried by all tiers: gentler (No Sweat / Slightly Sweaty) sessions still advise consulting a provider if symptoms or concerns are present.
sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion: the avatar honors the global reduced-motion setting and falls back to aria-live captions paired to every cue; every move is skippable. Chair/floor alternative path for this vigorous session: cardio intervals (fast-feet, lateral-steps, skater-steps, shadow-box) can be performed seated in a sturdy chair as marching feet, seated side reaches, and seated punches at the same work/recovery timing; squats become sit-to-stand from the chair; warrior2 and crescent-lunge can be done holding the chair back for support, or replaced with seated leg extensions. Floor-based core and wind-down (bird-dog-knee-hover, half-pigeon, legs-up) can be done on a bed or sofa, or substituted with the seated extended-exhale breath and a supported seated forward rest. The arrival breath and kind-close are already seated. Anyone who cannot meet the Super Sweaty screening gate should use the No Sweat or Slightly Sweaty 60-minute session, which need no floor transitions at all.
