sessions.intent: A gentle seven-minute reconnection for postpartum bodies: arrive with a deep-core breath, wake the spine, rebuild quiet strength through the glutes and trunk without any crunch or impact, release the hips, and close with kindness. The goal is showing up and re-establishing the breath-to-deep-core connection, never intensity.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome in. You have carried a great deal to get to this mat, and there is nothing to chase here, {name}. For the next few minutes, let us simply breathe and move slowly together. You are exactly where you need to be.
sessions.veraClose: That is it — you showed up for yourself today, and that counts completely. Notice how your breath feels now, a little softer than when you began. Be as kind to yourself as you are to everyone else, {name}.

items.dose: ~50s, roughly 5-6 slow cycles at 4s inhale / 6s exhale; settle seated or lying, no rest needed
items.tierNote: No Sweat arrival and core anchor. Chosen over box-breath because breath-led deep-core and pelvic-floor reconnection is the foundational postpartum starting point (pelvic health physiotherapy consensus on diaphragm/pelvic-floor co-activation). Restorative, never exertional. Kept near its lower dwell so the short session still reaches the close.

items.dose: ~50s, one full spinal wave per breath at ~5-6s each; slow and exploratory, no holds, no rest
items.tierNote: No Sweat warm-up tempo: gentle, breath-paced spinal mobility to wake the back and trunk before any loading. Low-impact, no end-range forcing. Warm-up compressed to a single move per the No Time budget while preserving spinal prep.

items.dose: No Sweat: 8 reps, tempo 2-3s up / 2s gentle hold / 3s lower, ~45s of work, 1 set; rest folded into the 6s transition
items.tierNote: Diastasis-recti-safe posterior-chain and deep-core work with zero crunch (explicitly cleared under the postpartum safety hard-rules). No Sweat dose: single set, slow controlled tempo, breath-led. Exhale on the lift to engage the pelvic floor without breath-holding.

items.dose: No Sweat: 5 reps per side, reach 3s / hold 2s / return 3s, ~45s total across both sides, 1 set; alternate at the halfway cue
items.tierNote: Sided. One of the safest postpartum core builders — anti-extension trunk control with no crunching, sit-up, or floor plank. No Sweat dose: low rep, slow tempo, wobbles welcome. Honors the alternating-sides cue so both sides are trained within the short window.

items.dose: ~50s total, ~25s per side, fully supported on the floor, still and breath-led; a slow breath between sides
items.tierNote: Sided. No Sweat held hip release after the glute work; restorative, not exertional, and not eligible for super_sweaty pacing. Wind-down compressed to one supported stretch per the No Time budget while keeping a genuine down-regulation before the close.

items.dose: ~60s, hands on heart, slow easy breaths between affirmations; this is the close, no rest after
items.tierNote: Mandatory affirmation close, never skipped even in a 7-minute session. Held near its lower No Sweat range (75s nominal trimmed toward 60s) to respect the time budget while still letting the kindness land. Completing this records the session and grows the garden +1 — consistency, never intensity.

disclaimers: This session is general movement guidance, not medical advice, and does not replace assessment by your own healthcare provider or a pelvic health physiotherapist.

Postpartum guidance: gentle, breath-led, low-impact movement is generally appropriate for most people once routine activity has resumed, but timelines vary; align your return to exercise with your own provider (consistent with ACOG postpartum activity guidance).
Stop and seek professional advice if you notice pain, urinary leaking, a feeling of heaviness or dragging in the pelvis, or any doming or bulging along the midline of your belly.
All work here is diastasis-recti-safe by design: no crunches, sit-ups, full floor planks, jumping, or impact.
Move within a comfortable range; pad the knees and use a wall or chair for support whenever you want it.
screeningGates: Standard postpartum disclaimer applies (this No Sweat tier carries no Super Sweaty screening gate): suitable as a first safe option, but confirm with your provider that you have begun returning to activity and have no unmanaged symptoms.
Defer or modify if you have unmanaged pelvic pain, ongoing bleeding beyond normal lochia, signs of infection, or any provider-imposed activity restriction.
Stop the session and consult a pelvic health professional if any movement provokes leaking, pelvic heaviness, or midline doming.
sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion: the avatar honors the app's reduced-motion setting and falls back to aria-live captions paired to every cue; every move is skippable. Chair/floor alternative path — Arrival breath and the close can be done fully seated. Cat-Cow can be performed seated in a chair, rounding and arching the spine with the breath instead of on hands and knees. Bridge can be replaced with a seated glute squeeze (gently press feet down and engage the seat on the exhale) if getting to the floor is not available. Bird-Dog can be done seated as opposite-arm/opposite-leg reaches, keeping the trunk steady. Figure-Four can be taken seated in a chair with one ankle crossed over the opposite knee and a gentle forward lean. No floor surface is required for any element of this session.

sessions.intent: A brisk-but-kind 7-minute moderate session for postpartum women. It opens with a seated pelvic-breath to reconnect the deep core, raises temperature with shoulder rolls, brings the heart rate up gently with low-impact standing marches at a talk-test pace, then builds diastasis-recti-safe strength through bridges and unilateral bird-dog before a short fold to down-regulate and a metered box-breath close. Conversational-but-working throughout: no jumping, no impact, no crunches, sit-ups, or floor planks.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome back. Seven minutes is plenty — we are going to wake the body up and do real work, gently. Come to a comfortable seat. Let us begin by finding the breath, and the deep core underneath it.
sessions.veraClose: That was strong, steady work. You showed up and you moved with care, and that is what makes the garden grow. You are exactly where you need to be.

items.dose: 45 secs — about 7 slow cycles, roughly 4s inhale / 6s exhale; let the belly and pelvic floor soften on the inhale and gently draw up on the exhale.
items.tierNote: Arrival breath. Compressed slightly from the full 60s to protect the 7-minute budget while keeping the mandatory seated-breath arrival. Doubles as the postpartum deep-core connection anchor before any load — restorative, not exertional, so it does not raise the tier.

items.dose: 45 secs — bigger, brisker circles than No Sweat, about 5s per slow circle, both directions, to raise shoulder-girdle temperature.
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty warm-up dose: fuller, brisker circles to prime the upper body. Single compressed warm-up move because No Time keeps warm-up short; this one prepares for the bird-dog/bridge work ahead.

items.dose: 60 secs at roughly 110-120 steps/min, arms swinging, then ~20s of that time bleeding into a brisk recovery before the next move; no jumping, stay conversational (talk test).
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty light low-impact cardio burst — the one moderate heart-rate driver in this short session. No-impact and no jumping per the postpartum ceiling. Conversational-but-working intensity.

items.dose: 10-12 reps, 2s up / 2s hold / 2s down, rest ~30s (compressed into the transition here).
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty bridge dose (single set given the budget). Diastasis-recti-safe posterior-chain and glute strength; breath-led with an exhale on the lift to keep the core connected. Alternated after cardio per the strength-with-mobility/cardio assembly rule.

items.dose: 8 reps per side (1 set), 2s reach / 2s hold / 2s return; keep hips level and ribs down, exhale on the reach.
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty unilateral strength — the DR-safe core-stability pillar (no crunches, sit-ups, or floor planks). Sided move; cued to switch sides at the halfway point. Single set to honor the 7-minute budget.

items.dose: 40 secs (Slightly Sweaty) — rag-doll hang with a gentle sway, knees soft, releasing the back and hamstrings after the strength work.
items.tierNote: Compressed wind-down: a single passive release fold to begin down-regulating before the breath close. No Time shortens wind-down but does not skip it.

items.dose: 75 secs — metered 4-4-4-4 (in / hold / out / hold), settling the heart rate and nervous system, finishing with a kind affirmation.
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty close dose (75s). Mandatory breath + affirmation close, retained in full despite the No Time compression because the arrival breath and close are never cut. Actively brings breath and heart rate back down after the cardio and strength work.

disclaimers: This session is general movement guidance, not medical advice, and is not a substitute for assessment by a qualified clinician.

Postpartum guidance: if you are within the early weeks after birth, have not yet had your postnatal check, or had a caesarean or complicated delivery, please obtain individual clearance before moderate exercise. ACOG advises that postpartum activity can usually be resumed gradually once medically cleared, with timing individualized to your recovery.

Stop and consult a pelvic health physiotherapist or your provider if you notice abdominal doming or coning, leaking of urine, a sensation of vaginal heaviness or pressure, pain, or any bright-red bleeding that increases with activity. [evidence: pelvic health physiotherapy consensus]
Work at a conversational, talk-test intensity. If you cannot speak a short sentence during the marching, ease the pace. [evidence: ACSM exertion / talk-test guidance]
Movement should not be painful. Reduce range, slow the tempo, or skip any move that does not feel right today.
screeningGates: Standard postpartum readiness: this is a moderate (Slightly Sweaty) session. Before starting, confirm you feel generally well, are not in the immediate acute recovery window, and have no active red-flag symptoms (heavy bleeding, fever, unmanaged pain, signs of infection or blood clot). When in doubt, complete a PAR-Q+ style self-screen or check with your provider. [evidence: PAR-Q+ / CSEP pre-participation screening]

Symptom self-check before and during: no abdominal doming/coning, no urinary leaking, and no vaginal heaviness or pressure. If any appear, drop to the No Sweat tier or stop. [evidence: pelvic health physiotherapy consensus]

Note: this session contains no Super Sweaty movements, so the Super Sweaty screening gate (12+ weeks postpartum, provider-cleared, no red flags, no leaking/heaviness/doming) is NOT required for this session. That gate applies only if the user selects the Super Sweaty tier.
sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion: the avatar honors the system reduced-motion setting and falls back to aria-live captions paired to every cue; every move is skippable. Chair/floor alternative path — Arrive and close: pelvic-breath and box-breath can be done seated in a chair or reclined. Warm-up: shoulder rolls work identically seated. Cardio: replace standing marching with seated marches (lift alternate feet/knees from a sturdy chair) to keep it low-impact and balance-safe. Strength: bridge and bird-dog are floor-based — if the floor is not accessible, substitute seated alternatives by squeezing the glutes in a seated bridge hold and performing seated opposite-arm/opposite-leg reaches for the bird-dog stability pattern. Wind-down: the forward fold can be done as a supported seated fold, hinging gently over the thighs. Use a wall or chair back for any balance support, and pad the knees for floor work.

requiredFixes: MINOR (non-blocking arithmetic): with all stated active doses summed (pelvic 45 + shoulder 45 + marching 60 + bridge ~66 + bird-dog ~96 + fold 40 + box 75 = ~427s) plus 6 transitions x 6s = 36s, true wall-clock is ~463s versus the 420s (7-min) target. estTotalSecs:427 reasonably tracks active-movement time and 'roughly fits', but to make the field defensible either trim bird-dog to 6 reps/side (~72s) or label estTotalSecs as active-work-only and note ~36s of transitions on top. Not a safety or design-soundness defect.

MINOR (copy/dose hygiene): the marching dose mixes '60 secs' with '~20s of that time bleeding into a brisk recovery', which reads as one continuous figure rather than a clean dose. Recommend stating it as '60 secs continuous at talk-test pace, then ease into the transition' so the active duration is unambiguous. The bridge dose similarly folds '~30s rest' into the 6s transition; reword to 'brief rest within the get-ready transition' so it does not imply standalone rest the engine cannot grant. Wording only — the intended low-impact, talk-test, single-set design is clinically fine.

sessions.intent: A short, honestly vigorous postpartum session for a screened, provider-cleared body: a quick full-body warm-up, one low-impact cardio burst paired with one closed-chain strength move to raise the heart rate within the postpartum-safe ceiling, then a deliberately longer mandatory down-regulation. No crunches, sit-ups, floor planks, or jumping at any point. Because 7 minutes is so brief, the main block is intentionally lean — one quality cardio-strength couplet rather than a long circuit — so the arrival breath, a real warm-up, and a full down-regulating close all still fit.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome back. Before we lift the pace, let us land for a moment. Sit tall, soften your jaw, and take a slow breath. This is a Super Sweaty session, so a quick honesty check first: you are at least twelve weeks postpartum, your provider has cleared you for vigorous exercise, and you feel no leaking, no heaviness, and no doming through your middle today. If any of that is not a clear yes, this is a wonderful day to choose No Sweat instead — that is strength too, {name}.
sessions.veraClose: That was real work, and you showed up for it. Let your breath settle all the way down and feel your heart find its quiet rhythm again. Nothing to chase, nothing to fix. You are exactly where you need to be, {name}.

items.dose: ~30s, metered 4-4-4-4. Compressed arrival breath that doubles as the screening checkpoint before vigorous work.
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty arrival is kept short (30s vs the 90-120s it runs as a Meditation/close) so the brief 7-minute budget can still hold a real warm-up and a full down-regulation. The arrival breath is never skipped, even in No Time mode.

items.dose: ~40s, bigger and brisker circles to raise shoulder-girdle temperature.
items.tierNote: Eligible at Super Sweaty only behind the screening gate. Brisker than the No Sweat lazy circle; preps the upper body and posture before the cardio/strength couplet.

items.dose: ~45s, larger brisker circles, switching direction at the halfway cue.
items.tierNote: Super Sweaty (screened) lower-body dynamic warm-up. Directly preps the hips and knees loaded by fast-feet and squats next; raising tissue temperature first protects joints and the pelvic floor before exertion.

items.dose: Super Sweaty: 20s fast / 20s recovery march x 2 rounds (~80s). Light, low-impact — quick feet, never jumping or impact.
items.tierNote: Super-Sweaty-only cardio. Chosen as the heart-rate driver precisely because it is low-impact (fast march, no jumping/impact), which honours the postpartum no-impact rule while still being vigorous. The 20s-on/20s-off interval keeps intensity controlled.

items.dose: Super Sweaty (screened): 12-15 invisible-chair reps with a 2s pause at the bottom, 1 set in this short session, rest ~20s into the close.
items.tierNote: Closed-chain, pelvic-floor-friendly strength paired with the cardio burst to keep the heart rate up. With only 7 minutes I run a single high-quality set rather than the full 3 sets; the bottom pause adds load without impact. No crunch/sit-up/floor-plank pattern is used anywhere.

items.dose: ~55s, 4-4-4-4, actively lengthening the exhale to bring the heart rate and breath back down.
items.tierNote: For Super Sweaty the down-regulating wind-down is MANDATORY and deliberately longer. Even in this compressed 7-minute session I protect a near-full minute of breath-led recovery rather than trimming it, because vigorous postpartum work must end with active down-regulation. A reclined option (legs-up) is offered in the accessibility note for anyone who wants a floor rest instead.

items.dose: ~80s, still, slow breaths between affirmations celebrating showing up.
items.tierNote: The fixed affirmation close, never skipped. Kept on the longer side (80s) so the session ends fully settled after vigorous effort; this completed session grows the garden +1 exactly like any other — consistency, never intensity.

disclaimers: This is general movement guidance, not medical advice. It does not diagnose, treat, or replace care from your own clinician.

Postpartum recovery is individual. If anything hurts, leaks, feels heavy, or causes doming or pressure through your middle, stop and rest, and speak with your GP, midwife, or a pelvic health physiotherapist before continuing.

The Super Sweaty tier is appropriate only once you are cleared for vigorous exercise. If you are unsure whether vigorous activity is right for you yet, choose a gentler tier today and consult a professional.

sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion: the avatar honours the system reduced-motion setting and falls back to aria-live captions paired to every cue; every move is skippable. Chair/floor alternative path — the whole session can be done without getting up and down from the floor: keep pelvic-breath, arm-sweeps, neck-rolls and the pelvic-floor and kind-close seated in a chair; do bridge, clamshell, bird-dog and side-lying-leg-lift on a bed or sofa if floor transfers are hard; replace floor figure-four with a seated figure-four (ankle crossed over the opposite knee, hinge gently forward) and floor low-lunge with a supported standing lunge holding a counter. No move requires lying flat if that is uncomfortable — substitute a supported semi-reclined or seated position.

requiredFixes: Tighten the closing pelvic-floor-connect cue COPY to carry the explicit hard-copy PF safety line at the cue level (not only in disclaimers/breath-plan): stop if bearing-down, doming, or leaking occurs; keep lifts brief and exhale-led; never hold the breath. Safety content is already present session-wide; this is a copy-authoring note for the not-yet-finalized new move, not a blocking safety gap.

sessions.intent: A moderate, low-impact 20-minute reset for postpartum women 30-40: connect the deep core to breath, raise a light working warmth through low-impact cardio and unilateral strength at a conversational "talk test" pace, then fully down-regulate. No jumping, no impact, diastasis-recti-safe core (bird-dog, bridge, breath-led connection) with no crunches, sit-ups, or floor planks. Built to feel like getting genuinely warm and a little sweaty while staying inside the standard postpartum safe ceiling.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome back. Let us begin seated, hands soft, nothing to fix. For the next twenty minutes you are allowed to take up space and breathe. We will warm up, work a little, and settle. You are exactly where you need to be.
sessions.veraClose: That was real work, and you showed up for it. Notice the warmth you created, the breath that is already slowing. Nothing about today needed to be perfect to count. You did this, and it matters.

items.dose: 8 slow breath cycles, ~4s inhale to soften / ~6s exhale to gently draw up, ~75s total
items.tierNote: Arrival breath is non-negotiable even in a short No Time session. Chosen over box-breath here because it doubles as the deep-core / pelvic-floor connection primer ACOG and pelvic-health physiotherapy consensus recommend re-establishing before postpartum loading. Restorative, so it is unchanged by tier.

items.dose: 45s, bigger brisker circles both directions
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty warm-up: larger, brisker circles to raise shoulder-girdle temperature before the standing-row and wall work in the main block. Compressed slightly from the 50s ceiling to protect the short No Time budget.

items.dose: 50s, generous circles ~6s each, switching direction at halfway
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty lower-body dynamic warm-up; moderate range to prepare the hips for unilateral strength and low-impact cardio. No screening gate needed at this tier.

items.dose: 45s easy-to-moderate march, ~100 steps/min, no rest into next move
items.tierNote: Light low-impact cardio to lift heart rate gradually toward the working zone. Marching keeps it impact-free (no jumping) — appropriate first cardio exposure for slightly_sweaty postpartum. Kept toward the lower end of pace as a warm-up rather than a main-block driver.

items.dose: 60s continuous, ~1 step-touch per second, talk-test pace, 15-20s rest
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty low-impact cardio opener for the main block — establishes the conversational-but-working intensity (ACSM talk-test guidance for moderate exertion). No impact, no jumping.

items.dose: 10-12 reps x 2 sets, 2s up / 2s hold / 2s down, rest 30s
items.tierNote: Diastasis-recti-safe posterior-chain strength and primary postpartum core/glute builder. Slightly Sweaty adds the second set and shortens rest versus No Sweat. Breath-led: exhale on the lift to engage the deep core without bearing down.

items.dose: 40s per side, controlled, exhale on each drive, 20s rest between sides
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty unilateral low-impact cardio that also recruits the deep core through the exhale-drive pattern. Sided move; the engine alternates sides. Stays impact-free.

items.dose: 12-15 reps per side x 2 sets, open 2s / close 2s, rest 30s
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty unilateral hip/glute-medius strength supporting pelvic stability — a pelvic-health staple postpartum. Second set is the tier escalation.

items.dose: 50-60s alternating, ~1 step-back per second, 20s rest
items.tierNote: Slightly Sweaty-only low-impact cardio (not eligible below this tier), adding light directional cardio between strength sets to sustain the working warmth without impact.

items.dose: 8 reps per side x 2 sets, 2s reach / 2s hold / 2s return, rest 30s
items.tierNote: The flagship diastasis-recti-safe deep-core and anti-rotation move (recommended over crunches/planks postpartum). Slightly Sweaty doubles the set count. Keep hips level, exhale on reach, no doming through the midline.

items.dose: 3 holds of 20-30s at a counter or sofa-back incline, rest 30s
items.tierNote: Upper-body strength as an INCLINE hold against a raised surface — explicitly never a floor plank, honoring the postpartum no-floor-plank rule. Slightly Sweaty uses a lower incline and 3 holds. Down-regulation begins immediately after.

items.dose: 50s per side, settled, breath-led, 6s transition between sides
items.tierNote: Held glute/hip release to begin down-regulating after the cardio-and-strength main block. Slightly Sweaty hold length; not a super_sweaty pacing move (it is a still stretch). Sided; engine handles both sides.

items.dose: 45s per side, twist a little further on each exhale, 6s transition between sides
items.tierNote: Gentle breath-led spinal decompression to keep lowering arousal. Slightly Sweaty hold length. Compresses the wind-down to fit the 20-min budget while still delivering a real settle.

items.dose: 85s, slow breaths between affirmations
items.tierNote: Mandatory affirmation close. Kept toward the longer end for slightly_sweaty (75-90s) to ensure heart rate and breath are fully settled before the session ends, and to anchor the show-up-not-perform message. Counts toward breathCloses / garden +1 like any completed session.

disclaimers: This is general movement guidance, not medical advice. You Got This! does not diagnose, treat, or replace care from a qualified clinician.

Standard postpartum note: if you have not yet had your postpartum check, or it has been fewer than about 6 weeks since a vaginal birth or longer after a cesarean, please get clearance from your provider before resuming structured exercise [evidence: ACOG postpartum exercise guidance recommends individualized return-to-activity, often progressing once medically cleared].

Stop and rest if you feel pelvic heaviness or pressure, vaginal leaking, abdominal doming or coning along the midline, pain, dizziness, or any vaginal bleeding that increases. These are signals to scale back and, if persistent, to consult a pelvic health physiotherapist or your provider [evidence: pelvic health physiotherapy consensus on postpartum red flags].
Move at a pace where you could still hold a conversation. If you cannot, ease off — this tier is meant to feel like working, not straining (ACSM talk-test guidance for moderate intensity).
screeningGates: Standard postpartum readiness: you are past your initial recovery window and have no current red-flag symptoms (no increasing bleeding, no pelvic heaviness or bulging, no pain). If unsure, treat provider clearance as a prerequisite.

No active diastasis-recti coning or pelvic-floor symptoms during the movements; if doming or heaviness appears, drop to the No Sweat tier or stop. [Note: this session contains NO super_sweaty movements, so the Super Sweaty screening gate (12+ weeks, provider-cleared, no leaking/heaviness/doming) is NOT required to run it.]
sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion: the avatar honors the system reduced-motion setting and falls back to aria-live captions for every cue; the engine already supports this and every move is skippable. Chair/floor alternative path: do the warm-up (shoulder rolls, hip circles, marching) seated in a sturdy chair with marching as seated toe-taps; replace step-touch, knee-drives, and step-backs with seated marching and alternating seated knee lifts at the same talk-test effort; do bridge, bird-dog, and clamshell on the floor as written, or substitute seated pelvic-floor connect and seated banded knee press if getting to the floor is not available today; wall-incline-press can be done standing at any waist-or-higher surface (counter, sofa back) — never on the floor. Figure-four and seated-twist are already floor/chair-friendly; the kind close can be done in whatever position feels restful.

Remove the stray 'No screening gate needed at this tier' assertion in the hip-circles warmup tierNote — harmless here (no super_sweaty content) but it contradicts the session's own correct screening-gate posture and should be deleted for consistency.

Restore the knee-drives breath cue, which is cut off mid-word at '...protects the pelv' — complete it to '...protects the pelvic floor' so the exhale-on-effort rationale is intact in the move metadata.

Consider capping to three by folding marching into the warmup-only role it already plays, and confirm no strength-superset rest drops below a full recovery interval (current 20-30s rests are the floor for a pelvic-floor safety variable).

sessions.intent: A time-efficient, vigorous-within-the-postpartum-ceiling session for a screened, provider-cleared postpartum body: raise the heart rate with low-impact cardio intervals supersetted with deep-core-safe strength, then deliberately and generously down-regulate. No jumping, no impact, no crunches/sit-ups, no floor planks. The arrival breath establishes pelvic-floor and deep-core connection before load; the extended wind-down is non-negotiable and longer than the warm-up to bring heart rate and breath back down. This is for someone who has cleared the Super Sweaty gate and wants to feel worked but safe in 20 minutes.
sessions.veraOpen: Welcome back. You showed up, and that is the whole thing. Before we move, let us land here together with one steady breath — feel the floor, feel your ribs widen, and notice your deep core wake up. Today we work, and we work smart. You set the pace, and every single option here is yours to take.
sessions.veraClose: That was real work, and you met it. Stay here a moment longer — let your breath grow long and slow, and let your body soften completely. There is nothing left to do now but rest. You are strong, you are steady, and you are exactly where you need to be.

items.dose: 90 s; metered 4-4-4-4. Seated or lying, settle and pre-connect the deep core/pelvic floor before any load.
items.tierNote: box-breath is super_sweaty-eligible (blocks: close/warmup). Used here as the mandatory seated arrival breath. Kept at the shorter end (90 s) for a No Time session so cardio/strength time is protected, but never skipped — it is the deep-core and pelvic-floor 'switch-on' that makes vigorous work safe postpartum.

items.dose: 45 s; bigger, brisker circles to warm the shoulder girdle before loaded upper-body work.
items.tierNote: shoulder-rolls super_sweaty eligibility is contingent on the Super Sweaty screening gate, which this session requires. Compressed warm-up for No Time, but raising shoulder-girdle temperature is needed before the wall press in the main block.

items.dose: 55 s; larger, brisker standing circles, switching direction at the halfway cue, as a lower-body dynamic warm-up.
items.tierNote: hip-circles super_sweaty eligibility is contingent on the screening gate (met). Mobilises the hips/pelvis before lunges and cardio. Sided:false, so no per-side doubling.

items.dose: 55 s; rhythmic low-impact side step-outs, ~1 step per second, with arm sweeps. Continuous, no rest, gradually raising heart rate into the main block.
items.tierNote: step-out-warmup is a cardio warm-up eligible for super_sweaty (gate met). Bridges the dynamic mobility into the cardio main block so the first interval is not a cold start. Strictly low-impact, no jumping.

items.dose: 12-15 reps x 2 sets (compressed from the standard 3 sets for time), tempo controlled with a 2 s pause at the bottom, rest 20 s. Knees track over toes, exhale and gently lift the pelvic floor on the way up.
items.tierNote: squats super_sweaty (screened) is the strength anchor of the first superset. Big-muscle lower-body work that elevates heart rate while staying low-impact. Cut to 2 sets to fit 20 min while preserving the screened super-sweaty intensity (pause + steady tempo).

items.dose: 45 s per side at a quicker, rhythmic tempo, 20 s rest between sides. Low-impact — one foot stays grounded; brace the deep core, no breath-holding.
items.tierNote: knee-drives super_sweaty is a low-impact cardio interval (sided:true, so ~90 s of work across both sides). Supersetted after squats to keep the heart rate up. No jumping; this replaces high-impact cardio for the postpartum ceiling.

items.dose: 10 reps per side x 2 sets (compressed from up to 3 for time), with a 2-3 s hold at full reach, rest 30 s. Keep the pelvis level, ribs down, no doming through the midline.
items.tierNote: bird-dog super_sweaty (gate met) is the diastasis-recti-SAFE core driver — anti-extension/anti-rotation load with no crunching, sit-up, or floor plank. Mandatory substitute for forbidden flexion core work. Sided:true. Watch for doming; regress to knee-down if the midline bulges.

items.dose: 45 s faster combinations at a steady, controlled tempo; soft knees, low-impact footwork only, no jumping. 20 s rest into the wind-down.
items.tierNote: shadow-box super_sweaty is the final cardio push of the main block — upper-body-led conditioning that raises heart rate with zero impact and no intra-abdominal pressure spike, appropriate for the postpartum vigorous ceiling.

items.dose: 40 s per side (slightly trimmed from the 50 s default for the No Time budget), back knee padded, breath-led and still. Releases hip flexors loaded during squats, knee drives, and shadow-box.
items.tierNote: low-lunge is a stretch (not super_sweaty-eligible as a held pose); per the rules it is used here in its slow held form to begin down-regulation, not at super-sweaty pace. Sided:true. Starts the mandatory longer down-regulation after vigorous work.

items.dose: 50 s per side, slow entry then a still hold, decompressing the spine after loaded work. A breath between sides.
items.tierNote: reclined-twist super_sweaty dose is 50-70 s/side as a spine-decompressing closer; chosen at 50 s/side to respect the 20-min budget while delivering the mandatory down-regulation. Sided:true. Gentle, fully supported on the floor.

items.dose: 75 s of still, supported rest with legs up the wall or on a chair seat; long slow breaths, let the heart rate fall.
items.tierNote: legs-up is described as the single best down-regulation closer after vigorous work; super_sweaty range is 75-120 s. Held at the lower bound (75 s) so total time lands near 20 min while still mandating real recovery. The longer wind-down required for super_sweaty is satisfied here.

items.dose: 90 s; in for 4, out for 6-8, exhale-led, to actively bring breath and heart rate back to baseline after vigorous work.
items.tierNote: extended-exhale-breath super_sweaty range is 90-150 s to down-regulate; held at 90 s for the No Time budget. Pairs the mandatory longer down-regulation with the required closing breath, parasympathetic-led.

items.dose: 75 s; slow breaths between affirmations, body-neutral, celebrating showing up and effort.
items.tierNote: kind-close super_sweaty dose is 75-90 s; held at 75 s. This is the mandatory kind-affirmation close that every session ends on — it grows the garden +1 like any other completed session, consistency over intensity.

disclaimers: This is general movement guidance, not medical advice. It does not replace assessment or treatment by a qualified clinician.

Postpartum return-to-exercise should be individualized. ACOG advises a gradual, individualized return to physical activity after pregnancy once medically cleared; vigorous and abdominal/impact loading in particular should follow provider clearance and the absence of symptoms.

Stop and seek guidance from a doctor or pelvic health physiotherapist if you notice any leaking of urine, a feeling of vaginal heaviness or pressure, abdominal doming or coning along the midline, pelvic or low-back pain, dizziness, or any unusual bleeding.

Pelvic-floor and deep-core symptoms (leaking, heaviness, doming) are signs to regress to a gentler tier, not to push through. [evidence: pelvic health physiotherapy consensus supports symptom-guided progression; specific threshold numbers vary by source — flag]
All cardio here is low-impact by design: no jumping, no running, no high-impact landings. Core work is diastasis-recti-safe: no crunches, sit-ups, or floor planks.
screeningGates: Super Sweaty gate (MANDATORY for this session): at least 12 weeks postpartum AND cleared for vigorous/abdominal/impact exercise by your maternity care provider or a pelvic health physiotherapist.
No red-flag symptoms at rest or on exertion: no urinary or fecal leaking, no vaginal heaviness or bulging/pressure, no abdominal doming or coning along the midline.

No unmanaged pelvic, abdominal, or low-back pain, no unusual postpartum bleeding, and no condition (for example unmanaged diastasis recti or a pelvic organ prolapse) that a clinician has advised against loading.

If any gate fails, do not run this session — drop to the Slightly Sweaty or No Sweat tier of the same length, which carry the standard postpartum disclaimer rather than this clearance gate. [evidence: aligns with PAR-Q+ / postpartum screening principles; consult ACOG postpartum guidance and a pelvic health physiotherapist for individual clearance]
sessions.accessibility: Reduced-motion: the avatar honors the app's reduced-motion setting and falls back to static captions; every cue is mirrored in an aria-live caption and every move is skippable. Floor/chair alternative path: the arrival box-breath and the legs-up, reclined-twist, and extended-exhale closers can all be done in a chair or lying down — for legs-up, rest the lower legs on a chair seat instead of up a wall. For the strength/cardio block, knee drives, squats (to a chair as a sit-to-stand), and shadow-box can be performed seated in a sturdy chair; bird-dog can be done as a seated alternating opposite-arm/opposite-leg reach if hands-and-knees is not accessible; low-lunge can be replaced by a seated figure-four-style hip opener. Cardio intensity is always self-paced — slow any interval to a march at any moment with no penalty.

requiredFixes: MINOR sequencing (prior concern #9, partially REAL): in the main block bird-dog (loaded deep-core driver) sits between two cardio intervals (knee-drives -> bird-dog -> shadow-box) with only 20-30s rest, so the diastasis-safe core hold is performed under accumulating cardio fatigue, the window when control/IAP management degrades and doming risk rises. Prefer placing bird-dog earlier (e.g., squats -> bird-dog -> knee-drives -> shadow-box) so core work happens while control is fresh, OR extend the recovery beat before bird-dog beyond 20s. Doming stop-rule and knee-down regression already cued, so this is a refinement not a safety failure.

MINOR accessibility completeness (prior concern #8, partially REAL): the chair/floor path names seated substitutions for knee-drives, squats, shadow-box, bird-dog, low-lunge and legs-up, but does NOT name a seated alternative for step-out-warmup. Add an explicit seated/marching substitution for step-out-warmup so every standing-cardio move has a declared accessibility path.

MINOR copy/data discipline (prior concern #7, partially REAL): the four PROPOSED NEW moves and extended-exhale-breath are legitimately not yet in exercises.js (confirmed), and box-breath is frozen at secs=75/blocks:[close] while this session doses it at 90s in the arrive phase.
